(This would be great to get @fmarier's and @pde's opinions on, among others!  And @paravoid, if you're still following this project.)

We need to figure out mechanisms for renewing certificates automatically, if a user wants to do that. This will require creating a cron job and also keeping track of what certificates should (if any) should be autorenewed.  This might interact a bit with operating system policies about the right way to create and manage automated recurring processes.

My current thought for Debian-like systems is this:

* When we originally install, we should create a `/etc/cron.monthly/letsencrypt-renewer` script.
* If the package is uninstalled, that script will be deleted.
* The script will read a file called `/etc/letsencrypt.conf` or `/etc/letsencrypt-renewer.conf` or similar. The default action will be to do nothing and exit immediately. (So, no actual autorenewal attempts will take place merely as a result of having the package installed.)
* If particular keys and certificates are identified in that file and a directive is enabled there to try to autorenew them, the script will attempt to do so (at an appropriate time prior to its expiry), and then try to deploy the resulting certificate.
* At the time that a cert is originally issued, the user will be asked whether or not to enable automated renewal of that cert.
* The user can manually edit the config file to change the policy with respect to any individual cert or with respect to this mechanism as a while.

How does this sound?  Here are some additional questions:

* Should we regenerate keys, or only certs?
* How to notify the user when renewal was successful or unsuccessful?
* How long before expiry to try to renew?
* Do we need to explicitly check an external time source when deciding whether to make a renewal attempt, or can we believe the system clock?
* How to structure the config file? What information belongs in it?
* How to decide what installer to use in order to try to automatically deploy the reissued cert?  What kind of API does the installer need for this?
* Is it OK to spontaneously send a web server a graceful restart request in response to the deployment of a new cert, without prior human confirmation?
* How to get the user to understand the possible need to back up key material or migrate it when moving a domain or server to new hardware? (Can we also provide a higher-security migration mechanism for people who are concerned about moving key material off of the system where it was generated?)
* How does this work on non-Debian-like systems?

issue_body:

And my routine, log_kafka_assignment, gets called pretty soon. But then it gets called at other times too, without my subscribing to any new topics or anything. Why does it do this? Description
===========

Currently CachedSchemaRegistryClient sends request to the schema registry by directly calling
get / post / put / delete methods from the requests package. This isn't flexible and provides no control over how requests are performed. Moreover this approach does not take advantage of features provided in the requests package like connection pooling for example.

My proposal is to refactor the CachedSchemaRegistryClient in the following way:
  1. Create a schema registry client which utilizes an instance of requests.Session to perform 
      requests. This instance should be provided to the registry client's constructor so that client code
      can configure it however it sees fit.
2. Build the caching schema registry client on top of that.

Let's talk about usage of **confluent-kafka-python** Producer together with Tornado web server.

We know that Tornado is non blocking-server and has event loop for processing socket events.
We also know that librdkafka has several threads and its own event loop. And confluent-kafka-python is build on top of librdkafka.

When we call Producer.produce() from something inside Tornado it executes in main thread. Right? Then librdkafka pass data (with callback, probably) to another thread (event loop thread). And when message succefully recieved by Kafka, callback executed.

What happens when librdkafka call callback function from its event loop? Does it lock Python main thread with GIL?
Is there any documentation around using custom partitioners with producer?

    >>> datetime.fromtimestamp(0,tz.tzlocal())

        if not ignoretz:

issue_title: Add video management to the Dispatch admin
issue_body:
We want to add an additional tab to the Dispatch admin that allows users to add, edit and remove videos. 

Backend requirements:
- Expose the `Video` model through the REST API

Frontend requirements
- Add a new set of pages (similar to [pages/Sections](https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/tree/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/pages/Sections)) for managing videos
- Add the video routes to https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/api/dispatch.js
- Implement video actions in a new file `actions/VideosActions.js` similar to https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/actions/TagsActions.js
- Implement video reducer in a new file `reducers/VideosReducer.js` similar to https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/reducers/TagsReducer.js

A separate panel/page should be added to the events section of the manager to provide an interface for approving pending events. This page should show a list of events that have `is_submission=True` by using the `pending=1` flag in the API call (see #562). After clicking on an event, the user should be taken to a page that displays the event information along with an "Approve" and "Disapprove" button. Clicking "Approve" should update the `is_submission` flag via an API call and then redirect the user back to the pending events page. Clicking the "Disapprove" button should delete the event and return the user to the pending events page.

issue_title: [Battery - Linux] Some AC adapters are named "ADPx"
issue_body:
#1483 fixed things to read the batter online status from `/sys/class/power_supply/AC*` (instead of from the battery).  Unfortunately, it looks like that last part (`AC*`) comes straight from ACPI, and is totally inconsistent across manufacturers; on my device it's called `ADP0` instead.

PR coming up shortly to add an item to the condition.  Alternatively, we can attempt to read from the battery's `status` file (in sysfs, next to `capacity`) and match against the string [`Discharging`](https://git.kernel.org/cgit/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/tree/drivers/power/power_supply_sysfs.c?id=refs/tags/v4.4-rc2#n50).

issue_title: Question:  How to deal with a field that could either be nested or a simple value
issue_body:
I'm working on a POC leveraging Marshmallow for marshalling/unmarshalling JSON to/from a remote key/value store.

In our case, the keys and values can either be simple or complex types.  

> key=1  or key="somestring"

or

> key={id=1}

I've reviewed the docs and perhaps I've missed a way to express this via the schema.  Is there a way to handle this situation?  Feel free to provide RTFM references.  Also, if there's a better place to ask questions of this nature, please let me know.

issue_title: Improve documentation for make_object data validation
issue_body:
I took me a long time to realize if I want to use strictly valid data in `make_object`, I need to use `strict=True` option.

Something like this:
```python
class LoginRequestSchema(Schema):
    login_name = fields.Str(required=True)
    password = fields.Str(required=True)

    def make_object(self, data):
        return UserLogin(data['login_name'], data['password'])

    class Meta:
        strict = True
```
because if I don't use strict, I got `KeyError` instead of the desired `errors` dict filled with messages on `.load()` call.
This should be emphasized in the documentation.
Or do I need to check for data validity in `make_object`? Also should I use something else instead make_object?

issue_title: Email address validation fails when mailbox name contains non-ASCII characters
issue_body:
Hi there,

It appears email address validation fails when non-ASCII characters are present in the mailbox name, which should be permitted as per [RFC 6530](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Email_address#Internationalization). See example (tested on ```dev``` branch):

```
In [1]: from marshmallow import fields, Schema

In [2]: class Test(Schema):
   ...:     email = fields.Email()
   ...:

In [3]: t = Test()

In [4]: t.load({'email': u'josé@blah.com'})
Out[4]: UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={'email': [u'"jos\xe9@blah.com" is not a valid email address.']})
```

Chars outside of ASCII seem to work fine in the domain though.

Thanks,

Lex

My model has 8000 iterations and 0.8 final loss.

I then validate it with some images using TC and bounding box drawing util and it recognizes them better that I expected!

I used iOS 12 beta 9 and Xcode 10 beta 6 as developing environment, with an iPad Pro 2017 (or 2016, I don't remember).

From my first test seems this could be a rotation issue, but I don't know if that is the real issue and eventually how to fix it.

issue_title: counting of epoch & trained size in resume training does not match original counts
issue_body:
By using checkpoint, the models/states are saved into disk periodically, and user can resume training from certain iteration (checkpoint).
 
However, in current logs, the same iteration maps to different epoch/trained size before and after resuming from certain checkpoint. This is confusing and could bring problems for people who need to combine and analyze the logs together. 

Problem illustrated below. Resumed training from Iteration no. 17149. 
This is the log before pause. The epoch batch counter for iteration 17149 is **[Epoch 13 0/1279867]**
```
INFO  DistriOptimizer$:230 - [Epoch 13 0/1279867][Iteration 17149][Wall Clock xxxs] Train xxx in xxx seconds. Throughput is xxx records/second. Loss is xxx.
```
This is the log after pause. The epoch batch counter for iteration 17149 is **[Epoch 12 896/1279867]**
```
INFO  DistriOptimizer$:230 - [Epoch 12 896/1279867][Iteration 17149][Wall Clock xxxs] Train xxx in xxx seconds. Throughput is xxx records/second. Loss is xxx.
```

issue_title: Error when creating a model with Replicate and a recurrent layer
issue_body:
When I run the following code:
```
import numpy as np
from bigdl.nn.layer import Sequential, Replicate, RnnCell, Recurrent, Tanh
bmodel = Sequential()
bmodel.add(Replicate(2, 1, 1))
rec = Recurrent()
rec.add(RnnCell(1, 1, Tanh(), isInputWithBias=False))
bmodel.add(rec)
bmodel.set_weights([np.array([[0.1]]), np.array([[-1.2]]), np.array([0.0])])
input_data = np.array([[0.2]])
bmodel.forward(input_data)
```
I get the result:
```
array([[[ 0.],
            [ 0.]]], dtype=float32)
```

However, if I run the following:
```
import numpy as np
from bigdl.nn.layer import Sequential, RnnCell, Recurrent, Tanh
bmodel = Sequential()
rec = Recurrent()
rec.add(RnnCell(1, 1, Tanh(), isInputWithBias=False))
bmodel.add(rec)
bmodel.set_weights([np.array([[0.1]]), np.array([[-1.2]]), np.array([0.0])])
input_data = np.array([[[0.2], [0.2]]])
bmodel.forward(input_data)
```
I get the result:
```
array([[[ 0.01999734],
            [-0.00399678]]], dtype=float32)
```

But basically, after forwarding by the `Replicate(2, 1, 1)` layer, the input `[[0.2]]` should be `[[[0.2], [0.2]]]`, so the above two code blocks should generate the same output?

issue_title: Provide references and a small comparison matrix
issue_body:
It's always good scientific practice to also give references to other freely available recommender  systems and maybe a small matrix comparing their main features. This has several benefits:
1) It allows new user to get a fast overview of freely available alternatives
2) It provides an overview of the main differences to the alternatives
3) Showing that there are alternatives and that their main feature are known to the authors of LightFM increases their reputation and perceived expertise.

I think the main alternatives are Mahout, PyFM and LensKit. All of them seem to provide only Collaborative Filtering. Interesting features would be: scalability/runtime performance, language bindings, algorithms/methods. 

I know that this issue could be quite work intense but it would be nice to have in order to convince people to use LightFM ;-)

issue_title: PicklingError: Can't pickle <class 'uuid.UUID'>: it's not the same object as uuid.UUID
issue_body:
Hi there, I've had an intermittent issue come up using Cachalot in our production environment (Apache, mod_wsgi, Python 2.7, Django 1.12)

Every few weeks we get this error "Can't pickle <class 'uuid.UUID'>: it's not the same object as uuid.UUID", from deep inside the monkey patch layer of Cachalot.  (Example sentry stack trace below).

The only thing that fixes it is restarting Apache. It'll then go away for a day or two, sometimes a week or two, then reoccur.

`
PicklingError
Can't pickle <class 'uuid.UUID'>: it's not the same object as uuid.UUID
cachalot/monkey_patch.py in _get_result_or_execute_query at line 59
`

Researching this, I've only found 1 other person with the same issue (unresolved) at https://github.com/niwinz/django-redis/issues/319.

Reading up on the pickling error, it seems that if you import a symbol in different ways (i.e. import x x.y versus from x import y) it results in this pickling error.

I looked through all my code to ensure that UUID was being used and imported in a consistent way, checking also how the Django Postgresql code uses it (as I use models.UUIDField for primary keys). Everywhere uses it as such:

import uuid
...
uuid.UUID

Except for this place in Cachalot, utils.py


from time import time
**from uuid import UUID**

from django import VERSION as django_version
from django.db import connections
from django.db.models import QuerySet
from django.db.models.sql import Query
from django.db.models.sql.where import (
    ExtraWhere, SubqueryConstraint, WhereNode)
from django.utils.six import text_type, binary_type, PY2

from .settings import ITERABLES, cachalot_settings
from .transaction import AtomicCache


class UncachableQuery(Exception):
    pass


class IsRawQuery(Exception):
    pass


CACHABLE_PARAM_TYPES = {
    bool, int, float, Decimal, bytearray, binary_type, text_type, type(None),
    datetime.date, datetime.time, datetime.datetime, datetime.timedelta, **UUID**,
}


I suspect this is the source of the issue here. I'm going to try patching this on our project with the following changes to see if it solves the issue (may have to wait 3-4 weeks before declaring it solved given the mean-time between failures).

suggested change below:

**import uuid**
...

CACHABLE_PARAM_TYPES = {
    bool, int, float, Decimal, bytearray, binary_type, text_type, type(None),
    datetime.date, datetime.time, datetime.datetime, datetime.timedelta, **uuid.UUID**,
}

issue_title: ModelChain.prepare_inputs fails to pass solar_position and airmass to Location.get_clearsky
issue_labels: [{'id': 177800477, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwxNzc4MDA0Nzc=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pvlib/pvlib-python/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'fc2929', 'default': True}, {'id': 961364188, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5NjEzNjQxODg=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pvlib/pvlib-python/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': '97f4ee', 'default': True}, {'id': 961364003, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5NjEzNjQwMDM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pvlib/pvlib-python/labels/help%20wanted', 'name': 'help wanted', 'color': '3bcc79', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
ModelChain.prepare_inputs calculates results for clear sky condition if no irradiance data is provided. The ModelChain is supposed to pass solar position and airmass data to Location.get_clearsky to speed up calculations and keep them consistent. #199 changed the ``ineichen`` API and this ModelChain code was not updated to reflect the new arguments.

[These lines](https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/blob/master/pvlib/modelchain.py#L772-L776):

```python
        if not any([x in ['ghi', 'dni', 'dhi'] for x in self.weather.columns]):
            self.weather[['ghi', 'dni', 'dhi']] = self.location.get_clearsky(
                self.solar_position.index, self.clearsky_model,
                zenith_data=self.solar_position['apparent_zenith'],
                airmass_data=self.airmass['airmass_absolute'])
```

should read

```python
        if not any([x in ['ghi', 'dni', 'dhi'] for x in self.weather.columns]):
            self.weather[['ghi', 'dni', 'dhi']] = self.location.get_clearsky(
                self.solar_position.index, self.clearsky_model,
                solar_position=self.solar_position,  # fixed here and below
                airmass_absolute=self.airmass['airmass_absolute'])
```

I am integrating a `django-rest-framework` backend and an `Ember` frontend (with `EmberData`). These are my versions:

  - Django 1.9
  - djangorestframework 3.3.2
  - django-rest-framework-json-api (latest version from github)
  - ember-cli 1.13.13
  - ember 2.2.0
  - ember-data 2.2.1

GET requests are working fine, but POSTs are not. This is the kind of request coming from EmberData:

```
{
  "data": {
    "attributes": {
      "name": "The project name",
      "description": "The project description",
      "price": 123
    },
    "relationships": {
      "onwer": {
        "data": null
      }
    },
    "type": "projects"
  }
}
```

But django is crashing, and reporting:

```
Page not found ... The current URL, api/projects, didn't match any of these
```

My URLs are created with a router:

```
from rest_framework.routers import DefaultRouter

from .viewsets.projects import ProjectViewSet


router = DefaultRouter()
...
router.register(r'projects', ProjectViewSet)
...
```

and `ViewSet`:

```
from rest_framework import viewsets
from rest_framework import filters

from vwrks.models import Project

from ..serializers import ProjectSerializer

class ProjectViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):
    """
    A viewset for viewing and editing Project instances.
    """
    serializer_class = ProjectSerializer
    queryset = Project.objects.all()
    filter_backends = (filters.DjangoFilterBackend, filters.SearchFilter)
    filter_fields = ('seller', 'artist', 'subcategory')
    search_fields = ('name', 'description')
```

According to the documentation (http://www.django-rest-framework.org/api-guide/viewsets/#modelviewset), `ViewSet`s provide support for `.create()`, so this POST operation should be supported.

I have also tweaked the trailing slashes for Django, in order to accept `POST`s at `/projects` as `EmberData` is doing (besides the default `/projects/` that Django is expecting)

```
# EmberData does POST to /projects (not /projects/), so we need this:
APPEND_SLASH = False
```

The problem that I see is that `Django`'s router does not generate a `project-create` URL (only `project-list` and `project-detail` are listed):

```
^api/ ^projects/$ [name='project-list']
^api/ ^projects\.(?P<format>[a-z0-9]+)/?$ [name='project-list']
^api/ ^projects/(?P<pk>[^/.]+)/$ [name='project-detail']
^api/ ^projects/(?P<pk>[^/.]+)\.(?P<format>[a-z0-9]+)/?$ [name='project-detail']
```

For this benchmark:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic ALL)
(define-fun i () Int (str.indexof "" "" 0))
(check-sat)
(get-value (i))
```

z3 says (both regular and z3str3):

```
$ z3 a.smt2
sat
((i (- 1)))
$ z3 smt.string_solver=z3str3 a.smt2
sat
((i (- 1)))
```

The correct answer should be `0`. This is corroborated by CVC4:

```
$ cvc4 a.smt2
sat
((i 0))
```

Also, the new string logic says:

>  ; Index of first occurrence of second string in first one.
>   ; (str.indexof s t i), with t non-empty and 0 <= i <= |s| is the position 
>   ; of the first occurrence of t in s at or after position i, if any. 
>   ; Otherwise, it is -1. Note that the result is i whenever i is within range
>   ; and t is empty.
>   ; Total
>   (str.indexof String String Int Int)

which explicitly says this case must produce 0 due to the last sentence.

Note that z3 is doing the right thing when the first string is non-empty. The problem only seems to occur when both arguments are empty.

issue_title: [CMake] _TRACE is not defined in a Debug build
issue_body:
@NikolajBjorner mentioned this issue to me recently. The Python build system defines ``_TRACE`` in a Debug build but the CMake build system does not.

This was a deliberate decision on my part and I want to explain the rationale:

The CMake build system provides the option ``ENABLE_TRACING`` which is off by default. When this option is enabled ``_TRACE`` is added as a compiler definition. This option is currently independent of the build type (e.g. ``Release``, ``Debug``).

It is not always possible to know the build configuration (e.g. ``Debug``, ``Release``) when CMake is run due to multi-configuration generators (e.g. Visual Studio). This means it is not possible force ``ENABLE_TRACING`` to ``ON`` when doing a debug build. However it is possible (using something CMake calls "generator expressions") to have the ``_TRACE`` define to be added depending to build configuration. The code for this already there but it is commented out

```cmake
# Should we always enable tracing when doing a debug build?
#list(APPEND Z3_COMPONENT_CXX_DEFINES $<$<CONFIG:Debug>:_TRACE>)
```

If uncommented this code it would lead to confusing behaviour because tracing would be enabled in a debug build even if ``ENABLE_TRACING`` is set to ``OFF``. This is why the code is currently commented out.

I can fix this but first I need to know

@NikolajBjorner @wintersteiger 
Is it ever desirable to have a debug build without tracing?

If the answer is yes then I think I should leave the CMake build system as it is to allow building a debug build without tracing. If the answer is no then I think I should rename the CMake option to ``ENABLE_TRACING_IN_NON_DEBUG_BUILD``. Then I would then set the build system to always define ``_TRACE`` in a debug build. The ``ENABLE_TRACING_IN_NON_DEBUG_BUILD`` would no longer be confusing because it would only matter in non debug builds.

The `TransposedConv2DLayer` currently produces an output of the minimum size that could have produced its input in a forward convolution. Sometimes that's not what you want: For a encoder/decoder architecture, you'll want each transposed convolution in the decoder to recreate the input shape of the corresponding forward convolution in the encoder. For example, convolving a 32x32 input with 3x3 filters, stride 2x2 and "same" padding will produce a 16x16 output, but applying the corresponding transposed convolution will produce a 31x31 output only (because a 31x31 input would have resulted in a 16x16 output as well).

To solve this, as agreed in #610, we should add an optional `output_size` argument to the `TransposedConv2DLayer` constructor that allows to override the inferred minimum output size.

It seems to me as if the pixels that lie on the boundary of the input to the TransformerLayer are smeared in the output.  I noticed this when I was playing around with spatial transformers by transforming MNIST digits and trying to let the transformer module learn to map the digit back to its original pose.

In the image below, the first row shows the input, the second row shows the output of the spatial transformer, and the third row shows the output of the spatial transformer after the boundary of the input has been set to zero.  Notice that in the second row smearing occurs wherever the input digit was touching the boundary, whereas the third row does not exhibit this effect.
![smearing](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6610704/15592112/145fe6c6-236f-11e6-9168-f484a225141d.png)


I suspect that the issue arises when the coordinates are [clipped](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/8fe645d28b66f991d547e9b6a314251b8e84446a/lasagne/layers/special.py#L470), because this leads to several locations in the output grid all mapping back to the same boundary location in the  input.

Here is some code to reproduce the issue.  You can get a sample of transformed MNIST digits [here](https://www.dropbox.com/s/ayxt5jkxtvekri3/transformed_mnist.pickle?dl=0).
```
import cv2
import cPickle as pickle
import numpy as np
import theano
import theano.tensor as T
from lasagne.layers import get_output
from lasagne.layers import DenseLayer
from lasagne.layers import InputLayer
from lasagne.layers import TransformerLayer
from lasagne.init import Constant
from lasagne.nonlinearities import linear

with open('transformed_mnist.pickle', 'rb') as f:
    X = pickle.load(f)
Y = np.copy(X)
Y[:, :, 0, :] = 0
Y[:, :, :, 0] = 0
Y[:, :, -1, :] = 0
Y[:, :, :, -1] = 0

l_in = InputLayer((None, 1, 28, 28))

M = np.array([[1.5, 0, 0.0], [0, 1.5, 0.0]])
l_loc = DenseLayer(
    l_in, num_units=6, W=Constant(0.0), b=M.flatten(), nonlinearity=linear
)
l_trans = TransformerLayer(l_in, l_loc)

X_tensor = T.tensor4()
X_hat = get_output(l_trans, X_tensor)
infer = theano.function([X_tensor], X_hat)

output1 = infer(X)
output2 = infer(Y)
row1 = (255. * np.hstack(X.transpose(0, 2, 3, 1)))
row2 = (255. * np.hstack(output1.transpose(0, 2, 3, 1)))
row3 = (255. * np.hstack(output2.transpose(0, 2, 3, 1)))
cv2.imwrite('smearing.png', np.vstack((row1, row2, row3)))
```

issue_title: Top level Immutable.js for app state
issue_body:
Our `state` should be an Immutable.js structure (especially since we use it everywhere else). Then we can nuke the whole `...state` return from reducers, opting for `.setIn`, etc. That object rest spread ends up doing an `Object.assign`, copying things along the way.

We can go full immutable, even though some branches of the tree are mutable objects. This includes, e.g., `channels`.

This is a follow up to #194

issue_title: Close execution based on other events
issue_body:
Our epic for execution will kill a cell's current execution off if we get an `ABORT_EXECUTION` action with the matching id (as well as if a new execution happens). There are other actions/events where we should expect to close those observables (and not update the display, etc.) It would be really good if this was tracked by the observable (we have the mechanics!) rather than endless bookkeeping all over the place. I'll go ahead and list the actions I'm thinking of and whether we handle that already:

* [x] `ABORT_EXECUTION`
* [x] `EXECUTE_CELL` (a new execution request for an ID comes in)
* [ ] `NEW_KERNEL`
* [ ] `KILL_KERNEL`
* [ ] `INTERRUPT_KERNEL` (?)
* [ ] `REMOVE_CELL`

Special cases I'm unsure of:

* [ ] `UNDO`

* [x] Kernel Selection - Top level menu?
* [x] Notebook(s) - Are these in individual windows, tabbed, selectable?
* [x] Notebook selection - is this a dashboard, a menu, or a sidebar?

issue_title: Append instead of replace parameter cell
issue_body: Cells overwrite the contents of the parameter tagged cell which is non-intuitive and easy to mess up. Instead it would be more reasonable if the tagged cell used values there as default values and simply appended its contents to the cell definition. Repeated names would still be overwritten and non-papermill execution could enumerate sane values without breaking papermill execution as it is today. But, it would allow for easier assignment of defaults without adding a new cell. Today all the notebooks I've seen for papermill leave a blank cell for parameters which has to be explained to each person who sees a papermill notebook as opposed to a normal notebook when there shouldn't be so much of a difference.

issue_title: Allow detaching player from the mpsyt terminal window

Feature request: give the user the option of running the video player without keeping the main mpsyt terminal window busy.

issue_title: Tiny maybe-bug in obsolete line folding handling
issue_body:
I was looking at https://github.com/shazow/urllib3/pull/1318, and realized I wasn't quite sure what the right rules were for handling whitespace in headers using the "obsolete line-folding rule". Specifically, if we have a header like `Name: value1${WHITESPACE1}\r\n${WHITESPACE2}value2` then what is the header's logical value?

Currently h11 treats it as: `value1${WHITESPACE1} value2`

That PR makes urllib3 treat it as: `value1 value2`

RFC 7230 says:

```
     header-field   = field-name ":" OWS field-value OWS

     field-name     = token
     field-value    = *( field-content / obs-fold )
     field-content  = field-vchar [ 1*( SP / HTAB ) field-vchar ]
     field-vchar    = VCHAR / obs-text

     obs-fold       = CRLF 1*( SP / HTAB )
```

(And then in the text it specifies that `obs-fold` tokens should be replaced by one or more `SP`.)

As written, this actually says that `${WHITESPACE1}` being non-empty is an *error*, and only `Name: value1\r\n${WHITESPACE2}value2` is legal (and it becomes `Name: value1 value2`). So h11 doesn't quite follow that.

The reason h11 doesn't quite follow it though is that the spec's ABNF is buggy – its `field-content` rule is just wrong: https://www.rfc-editor.org/errata/eid4189

The suggestion in that errata is:

```
     field-content  = field-vchar [ 1*( SP / HTAB / field-vchar ) field-vchar ]
     obs-fold       = OWS CRLF 1*( SP / HTAB )
```

This matches the urllib3 interpretation, but not the current h11 interpretation.

Of course, the comment on that errata is "this is not right", but OTOH "The [fix] is the best approach for now, and a better fix will be developed", someday, maybe.

So I guess ideally h11 should switch to strip trailing whitespace from header lines that are followed by `obs-fold` lines.

This is the opposite of a high-priority bug.

issue_title: use a dictionary for query params
issue_body:
`hyperlink` does not support dictionaries for `query`. while i know that technically this is an `orderermultidict`, but to be honest this is just not nice from an api perspective:

``` python
>>> url = URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2')

>>> url.replace(query={'foo': 'bar'})
[...]
ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 2)
```

instead, this works:

``` python
>>> url.replace(query=[('foo', 'bar')])
URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2?foo=bar')
```

or using `.items()`:

```python
>>> url.replace(query={'foo': 'bar'}.items())
URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2?foo=bar')
```

but tbh none of this looks appealing to me. :)

any reason for the current behaviour?

issue_title: Web UI livestate refresh and update
issue_body:
When Alignak is running the Web UI sometimes do not refresh properly. 

What should be refreshed:

issue_title: Taking action limits into account in PPO/TRPO/ACKTR.
issue_body:
This is more of a question than an issue. I noticed that in the implementation of the above-mentioned algorithms, action limits are not taken into account. Environments handle this clipping internally, so no errors will appear, but this brings us to situations where the algorithm in it's training batch has inputs that were not necessarily applied within the environment.

For example, let's say the upper limit for an input is 1 and the applied input (given from the algorithm) is 5. What the environment will experience is input 1, due to it's internal clipping, but the algorithm will in it's training batch have data with action equal to 5. 

Intuitively it makes sense that the algorithm will learn how to deal with this, but I am wondering if using the information of exactly what action is applied would be beneficial? Additionally, we can think of applying the action clipping even before adding noise (since noise doesn't really do anything if the mean is already out of limits). For example, DDPG handles this nicely with tanh outputs before applying the noise, and with clipping applied afterwards.

issue_title: Line charts become degenerate when runs are changed in other dashboards.
issue_body:
Visit the scalars dashboard. Navigate to the image dashboard, and toggle any run. Navigate back to the scalars dashboard. Note the squished line charts.

![6y1wqoxhkvs](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4221553/30679042-61a55b70-9e4b-11e7-8cbd-e02477f4f526.png)

Part of why this happens is that TensorBoard includes all active dashboards in the DOM. All are hidden except for the selected dashboard.
https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard/blob/c52bd79c7d99a306cadb5c203810cb10f3660c42/tensorboard/components/tf_tensorboard/tf-tensorboard.html#L202

Hence, the `attach` handler for each dashboard is not a proxy for whether the dashboard is selected. In general, there is no systematic way for TensorBoard to inform each dashboard of whether it is currently selected.

We should pass the summary description stored within SummaryMetadata into the `description` field of `tf-card-heading` within the PR curves dashboard. This is in line with other dashboards and lets the user view rich-text descriptions of each PR curve.

This task will require some reconciling because each PR curve is associated with a tag, and each PR curve can have multiple runs. In turn, each run-tag combination has a description.

The scalars dashboard has logic to address this problem that we should make available to other dashboards.
https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard/blob/ba2ef0a2dac5881f37f81a55f17e18b7f9008416/tensorboard/plugins/scalar/tf_scalar_dashboard/tf-scalar-dashboard.html#L295

Switching pages back and forward will cause this bug. But after a while when it automatically update the graphs, these plots will restore to the normal size.

 - TensorBoard version: 1.8.0 
 - TensorFlow version: 1.8.0
 - OS Platform and version: Linux Ubuntu 16.04
 - Python version: 3.6.5
 - For browser-related issues:
   - Browser type and version: Chrome Version 65.0.3325.181

issue_title: how to use tflearn with tf.Session()
issue_body:
Sometimes we have to use tf.Session explicitly like distributed tensorflow. Example foleder gives an example layer.py but not work correctly because some layers like tflearn.dropout and tflearn.batch_normalization behave differently at training time and testing time. These layers use variable "is_training" and tf.cond to distinguish between training mode and testing mode, but the defalut value of "is_traing" is False, so the dropout layer not work.

`import tensorflow as tf
import tflearn

x = tf.placeholder(shape=(None, 784), dtype=tf.float32)
y_ = tf.placeholder(shape=(None, 10, dtype=tf.float32)

net = tflearn.input_data(placeholder=x)
net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, 128, activation='relu', weigits_init='zeros')
net = tflearn.dropout(net, 0.5)
net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, 256, activation='relu', weights_init='zeros')
net = tflearn.dropout(net, 0.5)
net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, 10, activation='softmax', weights_init='zeros')

loss = tf.reduce_mean(tf.nn.softmax_cross_entropy_with_logits(net, y_))
train_op = tf.train.AdamOptimizer(learning_rate=0.01).minimize(loss)

sess = tf.Session()
sess.run(tf.initialize_all_variables())

for i in range(100):
     batch_xs, batch_ys = mnist.train.next_batch(100)
     sess.run(train_op, feed_dict={x: batch_xs, y_: batch_ys})

correct_prediction = tf.equal(tf.argmax(net, 1), tf.argmax(y_, 1))
acc_val = tf.reduce_mean(tf.cast(correct_prediction, tf.float32))
print sess.run(acc_val)
`

The result is exactly the same even if comment out part of droput code (Only the first 10,000 examles are used so no shuffle on data). Using tflearn.is_trianing(True) to set training mode but  still not work

`
sess = tf.Session()
sess.run(tf.initialize_all_variables())
print sess.run(tflearn.get_training_mode()) #False
tflearn.is_training(True, session=sess)
print sess.run(tflear.get_training_mode()) #now True
`

Is a correct way to switch between training mode and  testing mode?

190c190

issue_title: Email prompt says it is for "lost key recovery"
issue_body: This is confusing since we don't actually offer recovery of lost keys. We should remove that text.

issue_title: Dropping support for Pythons in fetch.py
issue_body: If we drop support for a version of Python previously supported by fetch.py, existing certbot-auto installations can fail. If we're unable to reboostrap because we don't have the user's permission, when certbot-auto is run again during phase 1 it will call `DeterminePythonVersion` and if we can't find an appropriate Python, we exit with an error. Instead, we should print a message about being unable to check for upgrades.

issue_title: Always save server value in renewal config
issue_body: Currently, if people use configuration files from 0.5.0+ and then use them with a version before 0.5.0, renewal fails because it expects the server value to be there. We should always save the server value in the renewal config to help mitigate this problem.

issue_title: "Cert not yet due for renewal", but it expires on 16th January 2017 (It's 27th Dec. today)
issue_body:
Basically "certbot renew" should renew all certificates which expire within 30 days. My certificate does (my site is www.adnanber.be so you can check the certificate there), but certbot insists that it is not due for renewal (I even got an e-mail notification saying that it expires in 19 days).

The output when running certbot renew is:
```
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Processing /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/adnanber.be.conf
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Cert not yet due for renewal

The following certs are not due for renewal yet:
  /etc/letsencrypt/live/adnanber.be/fullchain.pem (skipped)
No renewals were attempted.
```

A little block from my nginx file (/etc/nginx/sites/adnanber.be)
```
    #ssl
    ssl on;
    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/adnanber.be/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/adnanber.be/privkey.pem;
```

My renewal file (/etc/letsencrypt/renewal/adnanber.be.conf)

```
# renew_before_expiry = 30 days
cert = /etc/letsencrypt/live/adnanber.be/cert.pem
privkey = /etc/letsencrypt/live/adnanber.be/privkey.pem
chain = /etc/letsencrypt/live/adnanber.be/chain.pem
fullchain = /etc/letsencrypt/live/adnanber.be/fullchain.pem
version = 0.9.3

# Options and defaults used in the renewal process
[renewalparams]
installer = None
authenticator = webroot
webroot_path = /etc/letsencrypt/live,
account = [REDACTED]
[[webroot_map]]
www.adnanber.be = /etc/letsencrypt/live
adnanber.be = /etc/letsencrypt/live
```

I'm not sure if the first row is supposed to be commented, or if there's supposed to be a comma at the end of the webroot_path row, but I tried removing them and got the same error, so I put them back like they used to be.

Anyway, certbot has worked fine for me for the past 2 or 3 times I used it to create and then renew. For some reason it just suddenly doesn't work now. I might just have done something retarded but I do need some help and guidance in how to solve this issue.

I've also restarted my nginx server and my remote machine, didn't help.

issue_title: cli.ini boolean options set when False
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
linux alpine edge

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
pip install certbot==0.12.0

## I ran this command and it produced this output:

I created `/etc/letsencrypt/cli.ini` listing all options, even if they matched the default:

```
# /etc/letsencrypt/cli.ini

authenticator = webroot
webroot-path = /srv
dry-run = False
# ...
```

Command:

```
$ certbot certonly
```

Output:
```
<snip>
IMPORTANT NOTES:
 - The dry run was successful
```

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

I expect setting a boolean option to `True` would be the same as setting the flag:
```
dry-run = True
```

```
certbot certonly --dry-run -d example.com
```

I expect setting a boolean option to `False` would be the same as omitting the flag:

```
dry-run = False
```

```
certbot certonly -d example.com
```

The boolean flags were set regardless of whether they were True or False. The option just needs to be present in the configuration file.

## Here is a Certbot log showing the issue (if available):
###### Logs are stored in `/var/log/letsencrypt` by default. Feel free to redact domains, e-mail and IP addresses as you see fit.

As one might expect, different versions of Augeas behave differently. Being a central piece of the Apache plugin, we should create a Travis test that runs with Augeas version 1.2. This could be just running the unit tests, running compatibility tests, or both.

issue_title: python-certbot-nginx std install not restarting nginx after cron successful renewal?
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
Debian 9 Stretch

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
apt-get install python-certbot-nginx (without backport)
So I'm running v0.10.2 (and was wondering why it was so outdated until I cam to realise I didn't use the backport version)

Which leads me to the question: Is there any use to bother adding the dedicated PPA on Debian/Ubuntu? Or is using backport just fine?

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
I use the `webroot` style for authentication.

I added my own cron command for renewal and while investigating why I got an expired certificate alert on my website, I came to understand the existence of the `/etc/cron.d/certbot` 

In effect I just had to restart nginx for it to pickup the renewed cert.

A log analysis showed that it wasn't MY cron that did the last renewal, and thus explained why the`--renew-hook "systemctl restart nginx"` I bothered to add to it didn't kick.

So I'm wondering why this nginx restart hook doesn't exist with the provided `/etc/cron.d/certbot` ?
How is Nginx supposed to restart? Didn't I see the hook somewhere maybe?

I've now seen in the doc the `/etc/letsencrypt/renewal-hooks` structure, but it just doesn't exist with this version by default.

Is it just that this version is too old and didn't include the restart hook for the `webroot` auth method? I'd be surprised it wasn't part of the feature with a provided cron for cert renewal.

Also, it's nice to have an automatic log management (very nice indeed, and it is documented on the website) but it's not easy to understand when you're looking at the command line: I couldn't see any logrotate policy or any indication in the documentation as for where this rotation policy is implemented and configured. It'd be nice to have clues in the system (again maybe it's done differently in newer version?)

Thanks!

We should investigate this before releasing 0.13.0. If distros are successfully packaging `python-augeas` 1.0, we make sure Certbot still works with that version (once it's installed) and move our pinning to `tools/venv.sh` or modify `certbot-auto` so the necessary dependencies are installed.

issue_title: %chart does not support queries written in Standard SQL
issue_body:
%chart magic fails when queries use Standard SQL, regardless of whether the module referenced specifies dialect.

To validate:
When "myModule" is defined as:
%%sql 
SELECT a, b FROM [table]

The following works:
%chart bars -d myModule -f a,b 

However, if "myModule" is defined as:
%%sql
SELECT a,b FROM `table`

The same command (%chart bars -d myModule -f a,b) fails.

Either adding a dialect flag for chart or respecting flags from the module would suffice to solve this.

issue_title: User-defined parameters should be allowable in paths and tables
issue_body: Currently, this is only specifyable in BigQuery SQL. The paths and tables are parameterizable only with the built-in airflow macros around datetimes. We should enable user-defined macros here as well.

issue_title: Set up automated releases to PyPI
issue_body:
Travis CI supports setting up automated deployments to PyPI on tag builds. Using that would make life a lot simpler as we develop the library. Organizations like [JazzBand](https://github.com/JazzBand) base their open community policy on this capability.

We'll want to set up a `pylti` user on PyPI that will be used in the automated deployment process.
Use Sphinx, and ReadTheDocs.

## Problem Definition

Raiden may fail after a state change using the random generator is handled but right before the snapshot is taken. If that happens on the next initialization when raiden is recovering and applying the pending state changes a new secret will be generated and the resulting events won't match, this breaks the architecture model, since it's assumed the re-execution of a state change will always produce the same events.

## Solution

Removed the secret generator from the InitiatorState and add the secret into all state changes that require one, this way the secret will be serialized with the state change and the recovery will use the same /random/ secret.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Remove the Random generator
- [ ] Change the required state changes

https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/blockchain/net_contract.py
! this should be split in sub-issues
- [ ] define penalization
- [ ] allow for deposits on open channels
- [x] improve events and event data (not completely spec'ed yet)
   - [ ] generate the events
- [ ] refactor naming of methods&variables
- [x] implement `close`
- [ ] implement `settle`
- [ ] test lock-claiming

issue_title: Mediated transfers: disclosing received amount along with the secret_request
issue_body:
In a situation where there is a path: A-B-C:

A sends a locked transfer to C and A receives secret request -- does A know what transfer C actually got? For example whether C really got the amount A sent (excluding fees of course)? If so, how?

Heiko:

> hashlock is the transferid but A needs to rely on Cs word, that a certain amount was received or not. in practice it will work by C rejecting payments (CancelTransfer) which do not correspond to an expected amount.

issue_title: Define the maximum gas that may be used by a smart contract call.
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Some smart contract functions need to iterate over arrays (e.g. netting channel's unlock), the size of these inputs need to be limited by the available block's gas limit. Raiden must not assume the full block's gas limit is available so a different value must be used.

## Solution

I'm suggesting 40% of the total gas limit.

issue_title: Refactor tests to make it easy to run them in simulated multi-node mode.

issue_body:
Currently it is possible to overload the plasma manager by calling wait too much. For example, calling wait 1000 times on 1000 object IDs can trigger 

Assuming we start

```
rm dump.rdb; src/common/thirdparty/redis/src/redis-server 
src/plasma/build/plasma_store -s /tmp/s1 -m 1000000
src/plasma/build/plasma_manager -s /tmp/s1 -m /tmp/m1 -h 127.0.0.1 -r 127.0.01:6379 -p 29834
```

Then in python do the following.

```python
import plasma
import numpy as np

c1 = plasma.PlasmaClient("/tmp/s1", "/tmp/m1")

object_ids = [np.random.bytes(20) for _ in range(100000)]
for i in range(1, 1000):
  print(i)
  c1.wait(object_ids, timeout=10, num_returns=i)
```

This triggers (on the branch for #113) a timeout in the `object_table_subscribe` command in the wait implementation in the plasma manager. The timeout triggers a fatal callback.

To trigger that particular failure, you may need to decrease the timeout for `object_table_subscribe` command (otherwise, the manager will instead die via a SIGPIPE later on).

This could be addressed by not allowing timeouts or retrying an arbitrary number of times, but the real problem is the load on the manager (and the number of timers that are added to the manager event loop). Hiredis doesn't seem to handle large numbers of timers very efficiently.

A better solution would be to use Redis modules to write a custom Redis call to subscribe to a large number of object ID updates instead of doing a seperate Redis call for each object ID in the wait call.

It would be great if the autoscaler supported Google Cloud Platform.

I'm going to start implementing the GCP node provider soon. Thought I would open up this as a feature request to see if anyone else has done work towards this or possibly has any specific thoughts about the implementation (e.g. what should/could change from the aws node provider).

issue_title: Handle carbon copied addresses on tickets submitted via email
issue_body:
If a ticket submitted by email has CC'd recipients they are currently ignored. They should be added to the CC list for the Ticket that gets created.

See also GH-18. My feeling right now is that CC'd addresses should have _view only_ permissions to the tickets, but perhaps we can make it a configurable option.

The admin doc string indicates you can pass in a comma separated list.  I don't believe that mail_templated allows a string for recipient_list.  It should be a list of strings.  Adding .split(',') should be safe here.

Recipients takes a python list.

the command reference of Steps(z, nstep, refr, Fin) says

> Propagates the field a distance, z, in nstep steps in a medium [...]

and to me that is a bit confusing. I would expect that the distance z is divided into n steps, but apparently the Field seems to be propagated n times the distance z through the medium.

Anyhow, thanks a lot for the development and the python port of lightpipes, it helps me a lot!

Best regards,
Max

issue_title: How to set properly camera target?
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm trying to initialised my scene with the camera looking at a defined target and it does not really work as expected. Basically I need to initiate camera movement to have the view snap where it should have been at start. The following snippet reproduces the issue:

``` python
def renderer(width=960, height=540):
    grid = GridHelper(10, 20, '#000000', '#AAAAAA')

    ball = Mesh(geometry=SphereGeometry(radius=1), 
            material=MeshLambertMaterial(color='red'),
            position=[0, 1, 0])

    camera = PerspectiveCamera(
        position=[-3, 3, 3], fov=20, aspect=width / height)
    controls = OrbitControls(controlling=camera, target=(1 / 3, 0.5, 1 / 3))

    children = [grid, ball]
    
    scene = Scene(children=children)

    return Renderer(
        camera=camera,
        scene=scene,
        controls=[controls],
        width=width,
        height=height)


display(renderer())
```

Cheers,

Thomas

issue_title: embed widgets - not interactive on static webpage
issue_body:
Hi guys,

I've created a three "Select" widgets that observe one another. Basically, when one option is clicked by the user, the other boxes update their options accordingly based on a nested yaml file. Once the user selects all desired options, a button widget is pressed which displays the contents of a text file in the notebook (rendered to HTML with widgets.HTML). Here is an example of the code flow, just so you can get an idea:

### create widgets, with text options based on yaml file:
`top_level_select = widgets.Select(options=list(yam.keys()))`
`mid_level_select = widgets.Select(options=list(yam[key1]))`
`bottom_level_select = widgets.Select(options=list(yam[key1][key2]))`
`button_widget = widgets.Button(description='Retrieve Results')`
`text_widget = widgets.HTML(value='waiting for results')`

### define callback functions for "observe" methods
`. . .some code here. . .`

### observe methods to control text options
`top_level_select.observe(update_mid_level_select, 'value')`
`mid_level_select.observe(update_bottom_level_select, 'value')`
`button_widget.on_click(display_current_options)`

### display widgets
`VBox([top_level_select, mid_level_select, bottom_level_select, button_widget, text_widget])`

### here's how they look
![widgets](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4624246/28588782-aa4d00aa-7149-11e7-89e8-20fae0ae52e3.PNG)

Now, all of this works perfectly, and in a very small amount of code with is great. However, when I click the "embed widgets" menu option, copy the code, and paste it into an html file, the widgets are no longer responding to one another (options are not changing), and no results are displayed when the button is pressed. Could you help me to understand how I might get this to work? 

Thank you very much,
Allan

issue_body:
Hi,

I am running jupyterhub with default local spawner installed via conda (system-wide installation). Jupyterhub is installed in its own coda-environment which also includes the single-user notebook. I provide another system environment to all users called `py36-sys`. I installed the ipywidgets package from conda-forge in the `py36-sys` environment. Still, users get an error when trying to use the widgets. 


I also tried to run this command after activating the `py36-sys` environment:

```
jupyter nbextension enable --py widgetsnbextension --sys-prefix
```

but the error persists.


**Test code:**
```python
from ipywidgets import interact

@interact(x=(0, 100, 10))
def p(x=50):
    pass
```
**Output**
```
Failed to display Jupyter Widget of type `interactive`.

If you're reading this message in the Jupyter Notebook or JupyterLab Notebook, it may mean that the widgets JavaScript is still loading. If this message persists, it likely means that the widgets JavaScript library is either not installed or not enabled. See the Jupyter Widgets Documentation for setup instructions.

If you're reading this message in another frontend (for example, a static rendering on GitHub or NBViewer), it may mean that your frontend doesn't currently support widgets.
```

Since 6, Dec, 2017, after I re-build jupyterlab with command `jupyter lab clean && jupyter lab build`, jupyterlab behaves a little weird. At first, the page's height is only 180 px around, it cannot take my full screen of Chrome. Checked for a while, I found that @jupyter-widgets/controls seems at wrong order, the css override order is not as it was before. I wait for quite a few hours, then I rebuild jupyterlab. This time,  the page seems work fine, but when executing `import ipywidgets
ipywidgets.IntSlider()`, the output area shows that `Error displaying widget`. I wonder how can I specify npm package version, so that it can only build my own package, without updating packages like @jupyter-wigets/controls and so on? 
P.s. I noticed that @jupyter-widgets/controls have two new versions in last two days. But both don't work well. I just want to roll back to 1.0.10. 
@jasongrout  Could you give me some kindly advice, please?
Add HTML [accesskey](https://www.w3schools.com/tags/att_accesskey.asp) Attribute as ipywidget optional attribute in the form of shortcut_key property.

issue_title: Make use of the the centralised, asynchronous Redis cache

issue_body:
The tutorial and examples don't go into much detail on reaping.  As clients connect and disconnect from long-running nurseries, should we be reaping to prevent the monitor queue and zombie frozenset from growing forever?  For users that don't need any cleanup, "autoreap" might be a good option when creating a nursery which might automatically spawn a task something like:

```
async def autoreap(nursery):
    while True:
        batch = await nursery.monitor.get_batch()
        for task in batch:
            nursery.reap(task)
```
[more-itertools](https://pypi.org/project/more-itertools/) is required to run ArchivesSnake, and must be installed prior to running the example script
`rdoinfo` has grown bigger and `reqquery` for once did not scale well. I need to do some profiling and improve the speed, quite possibly on `distroinfo` side.

issue_title: Fix logging output that got borked recently
issue_body:
master rdopkg is prefixing its output with `INFO:` and also duplicating commands it runs in background.

Tix this before release!

If you set up the requests integration, you get two nested spans for each call to e.g. `requests.get`/`requests.post`.  I believe this is because opencensus wraps both `Session.request` and `requests.get`/`requests.post`/etc.: if I remember correctly in (nearly?) all cases the latter calls into the former, so we get one span from each.

Just in case it's relevant: I'm running into this on Python 3.6, Ubuntu 18.04, and I see it with both the `PrintExporter` and `StackdriverExporter`.

One is [self._map](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/tags/tag_map.py#L24), the other is [self.tags](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/tags/tag_map.py#L26).

Hi, 

We are trialing Atlassian localstack for mocking out Amazon Kinesis in our application.
I can start up the amazon services locally using the provided docker image.

I create a stream on my mocked Kinesis using the following command
aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4568 kinesis create-stream --stream-name "integration-test" --shard-count 1
I see the stream was created using the following command:
aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4568 kinesis describe-stream --stream-name "integration-test"
"StreamDescription": {
        "StreamName": "integration-test",
        "StreamARN": "arn:aws:kinesis:us-east-1:000000000000:stream/integration-test",
        "StreamStatus": "ACTIVE"
    }

The arn is arn:aws:kinesis:us-east-1:000000000000:stream/integration-test

I couldn't find any information in the wiki/readme how I go about setting up my credentials to point at this test stream? 
In my tests I can configure the AWS_DEFAULT_REGION. I'm just wondering does anyone know how to configure a global account to match 000000000000 above? It keeps picking up my real amazon account from my credentials. 
Also, I specify --endpoint-url in the amazon cli command above. Is it possible to set this globally so that it can be picked up by my aws sdk? 

Its probably obvious to most but maybe updating the readme with some instructions how to create these to point at the mocked service 

Cheers

issue_title: Fix qualified URL issues with new API versions
issue_body: qualified artifact locations contain more than one scheme after changes to endpoint logic within runner.py

Some of the operators (e.g. OptimizedRNN, see #149) require several inputs that can be independently present or absent.

Possible solution: allow some of the positional inputs to be 'None' in the graph with inputs represented as empty string in the proto. Schema checker would enforce that they can appear only after the MinArgs provided.

If not objections - I can cut the diff.

issue_title: Question how to override `__init__` methods of `HasProperties` classes while maintaining picklability..
issue_body:
I need to be able to override `__init__` method of the `HasProperties` classes that I am writing. I also require to be able to pickle the object. 

I would prefer to being able to explicitly declare arguments ( like `__init__(self, arg1, arg2,...)` ) that would correspond with the properties or assign the arguments to their respective properties.  but this will brake `pickle`. Below is a screen shot  example
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6052367/35590347-71f8aa2c-05bb-11e8-8998-d3077a547282.png)

I have experimented with using keyword argument to be able to pickle objects but haven't had success with it. I attached a ipynb.txt version of the notebook I have been using. 

I would appreciate some guidance/discussion on best practice of overriding `__init__` methods of `HasProperties`, while being able to pickle the objects. I am not clear on how using arguments in the `__init__` method integrates with the `property` ecosystem, so if there true benefit to converting to keyword arguments I could be convinced.

Thanks, 

[PickleWork.ipynb.txt](https://github.com/aranzgeo/properties/files/1679329/PickleWork.ipynb.txt)

issue_title: lax rdfa parser - when html5 parser missing, please suggest the exact library needed
issue_body:
danbrick...@gmail.com, 2009-12-29T13:38:25.000Z 
(sudo) easy_install html5lib ... worked for me, but I had to figure out
which library it wanted first. 

g = rdflib.graph.ConjunctiveGraph().parse('http://example.org/foo/',
format='rdfa', lax=True)

gave 'rdflib.syntax.parsers.rdfa.RDFaError: XHTML Parsing error in input
file: not well-formed (invalid token): line 104, column 600. Though parsing
is lax, HTML5 parser not available'
 as an error message. The library name 'html5lib' is worth mentioning;
perhaps also a reminder about 'easy_install'?

Comment 1 by ed.summers
huh i guess commit for r1730 didn't close the ticket :-(


Comment 2 by danbrick...@gmail.com
sure it does! didn't know you'd fixed already :)

issue_title: Cornice redefines the accept mechanics of pyramid
issue_body:
Pyramid already defines predicated arguments and we're redefining them in cornice, we should rather use them.

http://docs.pylonsproject.org/projects/pyramid/en/1.3-branch/narr/viewconfig.html#predicate-arguments
